meanings.definition: To bend the upper part of the body forward and downward to a half-squatting position; crouch.
examples: He stooped to tie his shoe-laces.
Can you believe that a salesman would stoop so low as to hide his customers' car keys until they agreed to the purchase?
to stoop a cask of liquor

meanings.definition: In a bent bodily position, hunched

examples: Deserts are known for being arid.
The millionaire viewed his gift as arid.

meanings.definition: A large number of birds, especially those gathered together for the purpose of migration.
To congregate in or head towards a place in large numbers.
examples: People flocked to the cinema to see the new film.

meanings.definition: Coarse tufts of wool or cotton used in bedding.
To coat a surface with dense fibers or particles.
Labour, work, especially of a grueling nature.
examples: Lindi has achieved her comfortable life only after years of hard toil.

meanings.definition: Characteristic of a spindle; slender and of weak appearance.
